title: A solid triangle with one arrow-shaped notch at its base corner. The triangle is the most structurally stable geometric form — it cannot be deformed. The notch is the only declared event interface: an arrow-shaped void that accepts exactly the governing `->` operator and nothing else. Precept's states are exactly this: a structurally stable form with one declared entry point.
conceptualBridge: The notched triangle means Precept's state is a structurally stable governed form because the triangle cannot be entered except through the one declared interface — the arrow notch — and the material prevents any other path.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.5 avg — the solid notched triangle achieves exceptional scores on structural soundness, legibility, visual economy, and monochrome test; the amber palette is warm and authoritative; the arrow notch is the most economical possible encoding of a 'governed entry interface'; only the conceptual bridge (3.5) prevents a higher score.
